name: A Coruña - Paseo Marátimo - Torre de Hércules
name_original: A Coruña – sendero Paseo Marítimo - Torre de Hércules
region_intro: Hiking around A Coruña offers a diverse landscape, blending dramatic coastlines with ancient forests and historical routes. The region features impressive cliffs, deserted beaches, and the unique Fragas do Eume Natural Park, home to one of Europe's best-preserved Atlantic forests. Coastal paths, such as those along the Costa da Morte, provide expansive ocean views, while inland areas offer verdant river valleys and unique geological formations like the Dunes of Corrubedo. The terrain varies from urban promenades to rugged natural sections, catering to different hiking preferences. Best hiking trails around A Coruña The most popular hiking route is A Coruña - Paseo Marátimo - Torre de Hércules, a 5.5 miles (8.8 km) trail that takes 2 hours 19 minutes to complete. This moderate route follows the city's promenade, offering views of the Atlantic Ocean and the historic Tower of Hercules. Another top favourite among local hikers is A Coruña Seafront Promenade – Lapas Beach loop from Estadio ABANCA-RIAZOR, a moderate 8.1 miles (13.0 km) path. This loop explores the extensive seafront promenade, connecting urban beaches and coastal scenery. Local hikers also love the Lapas Beach – Tower of Hercules loop from Estadio ABANCA-RIAZOR, a 6.9 miles (11.1 km) trail leading through coastal areas and past significant landmarks, often completed in about 2 hours 57 minutes. Hiking around A Coruña is defined by coastal paths, ancient forests, and dramatic cliffs. The network offers options for different ability levels, from urban strolls to more challenging natural trails. The routes in A Coruña are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 300 reviews. More than 3000 hikers have used komoot to explore A Coruña's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in A Coruña?
A: A Coruña offers a wide variety of hiking experiences, with over 100 routes available on komoot. These range from easy urban strolls to more challenging natural trails, ensuring options for all skill levels.

Q: What types of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in A Coruña?
A: A Coruña boasts incredibly diverse landscapes. You can explore the dramatic &lt;strong&gt;Costa da Morte&lt;/strong&gt; with its ocean waters, deserted beaches, and impressive cliffs, or immerse yourself in the ancient &lt;strong&gt;Fragas do Eume Natural Park&lt;/strong&gt;, one of Europe's best-preserved Atlantic forests. The region also features unique geological formations like the &lt;strong&gt;Dunes of Corrubedo Natural Park&lt;/strong&gt; and the stunning &lt;strong&gt;Ézaro Waterfall&lt;/strong&gt;, where the Xallas River plunges directly into the Atlantic.

Q: Are there any easy, family-friendly hiking trails in A Coruña?
A: Yes, A Coruña has many easy routes suitable for families. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40330758" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de San Antón – View of the Port of A Coruña loop from A Coruña&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 6.7 km trail, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40337091" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;City Beach of A Coruña – Domus Museum loop from A Coruña&lt;/a&gt; is another easy 6.0 km option, both offering pleasant walks with scenic views.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in A Coruña?
A: Absolutely. Many trails in A Coruña are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24647276" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;A Coruña Seafront Promenade – Lapas Beach loop from Estadio ABANCA-RIAZOR&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 13.0 km path that explores the extensive promenade and coastal scenery.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions I can see along the hiking trails?
A: A Coruña's trails often pass by significant landmarks. You can hike around the iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1093101" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tower of Hercules&lt;/a&gt;, a UNESCO World Heritage site, or explore the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3532496" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de San Antón&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3093759" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Carlos Garden&lt;/a&gt; also offers a beautiful historical site to visit.

Q: Are there any trails that offer impressive viewpoints?
A: Yes, A Coruña is known for its stunning viewpoints. The &lt;strong&gt;Costa da Morte&lt;/strong&gt; trails provide overwhelming ocean views, and the &lt;strong&gt;Vixía de Herbeira Cliffs&lt;/strong&gt; offer expansive vistas. The &lt;strong&gt;Monte Pindo (Moa Summit)&lt;/strong&gt; also features beautiful signposted trails with great panoramic views.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in A Coruña?
A: A Coruña offers hiking opportunities year-round, but the best time often depends on your preference. Spring and early autumn provide pleasant temperatures and lush landscapes. Summer is ideal for coastal walks, while the ancient forests like &lt;strong&gt;Fragas do Eume&lt;/strong&gt; are particularly atmospheric in autumn with changing foliage.

Q: Are there any trails that feature waterfalls?
A: Yes, the region is home to the spectacular &lt;strong&gt;Ézaro Waterfall&lt;/strong&gt;, where the Xallas River dramatically plunges into the Atlantic Ocean. While not a traditional hiking trail to a waterfall, the area offers beautiful views and is a significant natural attraction worth visiting.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in A Coruña?
A: The hiking experiences in A Coruña are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 300 reviews. Hikers often praise the region's diverse terrain, from the dramatic coastal paths and ocean views to the serene ancient forests and well-maintained urban promenades. The blend of natural beauty and historical landmarks is frequently highlighted.

Q: Are there any unique natural features to explore in A Coruña?
A: Beyond the well-known coastlines and forests, A Coruña features unique natural wonders like the &lt;strong&gt;Dunes of Corrubedo Natural Park&lt;/strong&gt;, home to the largest mobile dune in the northwest Iberian Peninsula. You can explore this area via wooden walkways, observing its freshwater and saltwater lagoons and rich biodiversity. Another hidden gem is &lt;strong&gt;Praia de Teixidelo&lt;/strong&gt;, Galicia's only black sand beach of non-volcanic origin, accessible via scenic hikes.

Q: Can I reach hiking trails using public transport in A Coruña?
A: While specific public transport options vary by trail, many urban and coastal routes in A Coruña are accessible from the city center, which is well-served by public transport. For trails further afield, it's advisable to check local bus schedules or consider car access, especially for natural parks like Fragas do Eume or the Costa da Morte.

Q: Are there any trails suitable for beginners?
A: Yes, A Coruña has numerous trails perfect for beginners. Many of the urban promenades and shorter loops offer gentle terrain and easy navigation. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40330758" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de San Antón – View of the Port of A Coruña loop from A Coruña&lt;/a&gt; is an easy route that provides a pleasant introduction to hiking in the area.
tips: The garden is one of the most unique green spaces in the city, in the Old City. It is a "hortus conclusus" created around the tomb of General Sir John Moore, hero of the battle of Elviña against the French and who died in 1809.

The Convent of San Francisco is located in the city of A Coruña and its ruins have been declared a National Monument since 1939. The church is still standing, located after being moved stone by stone to Paseo das Pontes, but from the original convent only its ruins remain after its destruction in the 16th century.

The strange name of Campo da Rata, where the monument is located, seems the most appropriate to designate the space where many citizens were retaliated with death during the Franco dictatorship. It is a tribute to the victims of the civil war and is linked to the old Provincial Prison, which is also located in the Torre field.

Hercules was on the ship with Jason and the Argonauts but never reached Colchis, since he landed together with Polyphemus on the island of Mysia in search of his friend Hylas kidnapped by the nymphs and, according to legend, none of the three returned To the ship

The Tower of Hercules is the oldest Roman lighthouse in the world and the only one still in use. Built in all probability in the second half of the 1st century by an architect from Coimbra named Gaio Sevio Lupo, its light has always been a point of reference for sailors. 
The current exterior covering is from the 18th century, the work of Eustaquio Giannini.
Small but nice beach next to the Tower of Hercules area

The surfers' fountain is located between Matadero beach and Orzán beach, in front of the Meliá María Pita Hotel and together with the Cuatro Caminos fountain, it is one of the best known in the city.

In it there are two sculptures of two bronze men surfing, made by José Castiñeiras, which pays tribute to the surfers who surf in this area.

waypoints.name: Ruins of the Convent of San Francisco
Monument to the Victims of the Spanish Civil War (Campo da Rata)
